summary: Iteration 10 frontend-only content/brand audit for 'The Web' boutique online school. Tested all 17 review items across desktop (1920x1080) and mobile (430x900) via public REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL. 15/17 PASS as-specified; 2 minor copy deviations where the code value differs from the spec value (both code values are internally consistent with surrounding copy, so likely intentional). Zero console errors, zero /api/* network calls (static site confirmed). All 6 new program detail routes (/programs/english, /maths, /science, /economics, /environmental-management, /travel-and-tourism) load successfully with hero + pillars + week + gallery (4 images each); legacy slug /programs/lower-school correctly shows 'Thread not found'. Nav brand='The Web / Boutique Online School', mobile drawer brand='The Web / Online School', footer shows country list 'India · UK · Egypt · Yemen · East Africa · Sri Lanka · UAE' and credit 'woven by Naqiyah Burhanuddin'. Admissions form submits with toast 'Inquiry received — we'll reach out within two school days.' Tel link +91 95378 88852 is present and correctly formatted as tel:+919537888852. Timeline has exactly 5 milestones (milestone-0 through milestone-4) under 'Our Vision · 02b' / 'Five what-ifs we live by.' Subject dropdown contains all 7 expected options including 'Full IGCSE (all six)'. All 5 events and all 5 story slugs match spec exactly.

issues: Stat #1 renders '7 Countries represented' (code: Stat n={7}), but review spec item says '5 Countries represented'. Current value is consistent with 'seven countries' used in hero description, footer ('India · UK · Egypt · Yemen · East Africa · Sri Lanka · UAE' = 7), and contact section; spec item likely stale. Recommend confirming intended number with source PDF.

H2 reads 'Six programs. One web.' in /app/frontend/src/components/Sections.jsx line 221; review spec item says 'Six subjects. One web.' Minor copy deviation — recommend updating 'programs' → 'subjects' if spec is authoritative.

action_items: Decide on Hero stat: keep '7 Countries' (consistent with rest of copy) or change to '5 Countries' per spec. If '5', update Sections.jsx Hero line 120: <Stat n={5} l="Countries represented" />.
Update Academics H2 in Sections.jsx line 221 from 'Six programs. One web.' → 'Six subjects. One web.' if spec copy is authoritative.
critical_code_review_comments: Sections.jsx Stat for grades uses a count-up from 0 that renders '6–NaN' or partial values mid-animation because format is `(v) => `6–${Math.round(v)}`` while `n=10` — animates from 0 so mid-frames show '6–1', '6–4', etc. UX-wise acceptable but worth noting as a potentially jarring count-up for a range field.

Sections.jsx line 121: ratio Stat uses `suffix=":1"` plus a format fn that returns `1:${Math.round(v)}`, producing final text '1:8:1' (redundant ':1' suffix appended to formatted '1:8'). Visible in the rendered DOM as '1:8:1Student-teacher ratio'. Recommend removing the `suffix=":1"` prop since the format function already produces the full '1:8' string.
Minor: program cards use `IGCSE 6–10` chip text but route slug `travel-and-tourism` and `environmental-management` hrefs are correctly set; no dead links detected.
Pre-existing: MobileDrawer useEffect cleanup on line 35 uses short-circuit with side effect — readability nit (already noted in iter 9).
Pre-existing: no backdrop-click-to-close on mobile drawer overlay.

success_rate.frontend: 15/17 items exact-match, 2/17 minor copy deviations; all functional flows (navigation, program detail routing, forms, toasts, mobile drawer) 100% pass

seed_data_creation: None — static site, no /api calls observed during full interaction test.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 10 validated The Web brand rewrite across all sections. Content & routing are correct; the only items worth touching are two optional copy tweaks (Hero '5 vs 7 Countries' and Academics 'programs vs subjects'). If those are changed, just re-run the desktop content assertions in this report's script (paste into mcp_browser_automation on https://web-academy-design.preview.emergentagent.com). The Stat ratio rendering bug ('1:8:1Student-teacher ratio') is a real visual defect — see critical_code_review_comments #2 for fix. Program detail pages use programsCatalog with slugs english/maths/science/economics/environmental-management/travel-and-tourism; all render hero image + pillars list + weekly rhythm + 3-image gallery. Legacy slugs (lower-school, middle-school, arts-conservatory, etc.) correctly 404 with 'Thread not found.'
rca of the issue: N/A — no bugs introduced in this iteration. The two flagged items are copy deviations between spec and implementation, not functional defects. The '1:8:1' visible ratio text is a pre-existing double-suffix bug in Stat component usage (suffix=':1' combined with a format fn that already emits '1:8').
